title: inga nobilis subsp. quaternata
intro: Inga nobilis subsp. quaternata is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae, commonly known as the Ingá tree, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Inga nobilis subsp. quaternata has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and boost the immune system. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in various herbal preparations, such as tinctures and capsules, to harness its medicinal benefits.
